findings.title: learn:execute claims phase/plan upgrades persist via learn:update-progress, but ccw learn:update-progress cannot receive in-memory plan changes
findings.snippet: // Persist happens via ccw learn:update-progress when we mark KPs.
findings.impact: Legacy plan upgrade (adding phases) is not actually persisted; phase-gating can be unreliable or silently ineffective.
findings.recommendation: Move phase assignment to learn:plan (always produce phases), or add a dedicated CLI API to persist plan patches (e.g. ccw learn:write-plan).

findings.title: learn:execute interpolates sessionId into bash command without quoting/escaping
findings.impact: If sessionId is user-controlled and passed to a shell, it can break parsing or enable command injection before the CLI validates it.
findings.recommendation: Strictly validate/normalize IDs before shell interpolation and quote args; ideally avoid shell interpolation by using file-based inputs or a helper that escapes args.

findings.title: learn:plan uses non-portable bash commands (cat/mv) in doc-as-runtime code
findings.impact: On non-POSIX shells (or restricted sandboxes) plan promotion can fail even if validation passes.
findings.recommendation: Replace cat/mv with node fs operations (rename) or write final file via Write() to avoid shell dependencies.

areas_remaining: Cross-check doc claims against tests and CLI behavior

leads.description: Inspect ccw learn:update-progress interface: can it persist plan patches?
